sentences.english: Becca! I've just realised I forgot to bring a pen today.
sentences.korean: 베카! 오늘 펜 가져오는 걸 잊었다는 걸 방금 깨달았어요.

sentences.english: Hey, no worries, Feifei, I've got you.
You can borrow one of mine.
sentences.korean: 내가 있잖아. 내 거 하나 빌려 써.

sentences.english: 'You've got me' comes from the phrase 'I've got your back', which literally means to support someone.
sentences.korean: 'you've got me'는 'I've got your back'에서 왔어요. 말 그대로 누군가를 지지한다는 뜻이에요.

sentences.english: We often use the expression 'I've got you' metaphorically to show support or offer help.
sentences.korean: 'I've got you'를 비유적으로 지지나 도움을 제공할 때 자주 써요.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?

sentences.english: Oh, do you need to leave the office early? Don't worry, I'll finish your work. I've got you.
sentences.korean: 사무실에서 일찍 가야 해요? 걱정 마요, 제가 끝낼게요. 내가 있잖아요.

sentences.english: I was in the supermarket and an old lady asked me to reach something high up on a shelf, and I said, 'Sure, I've got you.'
sentences.korean: 슈퍼마켓에 있었는데 할머니가 선반 위에 있는 걸 꺼내달라고 해서 '물론이죠, 제가 해드릴게요'라고 했어요.

sentences.english: You're scared of heights? Don't worry, just hold onto my arm. I've got you, you'll be fine.
sentences.korean: 높은 곳이 무서워요? 걱정 마요, 내 팔 잡아요. 내가 있으니까 괜찮을 거예요.

sentences.english: Sometimes 'got you' can be pronounced as 'gotchu', especially in some British English accents.
sentences.korean: 'got you'는 때때로 'gotchu'로 발음될 수 있어요. 특히 일부 영국 영어 억양에서요.

sentences.english: It happens when the letter 't' is followed by a /j/ sound, like in 'Tuesday', 'tuna' and 'nature'.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Throw one's toys out the pram
